title: expand_vars builds output string unconditionally
description: expand_vars() (evaluator.rs:685-732) allocates out = String::with_capacity(s.len()) and accumulates characters into it, but only returns Cow::Owned(out) when modified==true. When the string contains '$' or '\' but no valid substitution occurs, the function builds the full copy into out and then discards it, returning Cow::Borrowed(s). The modified flag is set only on actual substitution, making the invariant non-obvious: a reader must trace both branches to confirm that out and s are always identical when modified==false. Suggested rewrite: buffer should only be allocated on first confirmed substitution, using an Option<String> that is None until a difference is found, avoiding both the wasted copy and the flag-tracking complexity.
